my_thoughts: 'How do I tell which is which?'—they want to apply the distinction. Teacher gives felt markers: anxiety = scattered/tense, rage = focused/powerful. This teaches somatic differentiation. Next time they feel something, they can check: scattered or focused? That tells them: defending (anxiety) or feeling (rage). Two-turn teaching gives them both concept and application. Consolidation complete.
accumulated_insight: T2 (Final): Student asks 'How do I tell which is which?'—accepted distinction, now seeks application method. Teacher provides markers: anxiety feels scattered/tense, rage feels focused/energized. Two-turn consolidation: establish distinction (T1) then provide diagnostic criteria (T2). Student now has both concept (rage vs anxiety) and tools to identify each.
visible_response: And now we see that the situation with the doctors was echoing the situation with your mother...
